summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of static portfolio for Krishna Vamsi Gangisetty across desktop (1920), tablet (768) and mobile (390) viewports. PDF reachability also verified. All 14 acceptance items from the review request pass: hero (status pill, headline 'Krishna Vamsi.', tagline, metrics 1M+/99.9%/60%/3+), all 4 hero CTAs (resume opens /Krishna_Vamsi_Resume.pdf in new tab, mailto, GitHub, LinkedIn correct), sticky nav with blur-after-scroll, 5 nav links smooth-scrolling with active-amber highlighting, nav-resume-button, mobile hamburger menu with all 5 links + resume that closes on click and scrolls, About (2 paragraphs), Experience card with 9 bullets, 4 project cards, 2 Skills columns with all 12 expected groups (SDE + GenAI), Education + Publication cards, Contact copy-to-clipboard with 'Copied' feedback, mailto + social links, Footer with year 2026 and 3 social links. No console errors. No horizontal scroll at 1920, 768 or 390 px. Projects bento grid stacks to single column on mobile (card1 top=500, card2 top=1371). Skills columns stack vertically on mobile. PDF endpoint GET /Krishna_Vamsi_Resume.pdf returns HTTP 200 with content-type application/pdf (125749 bytes).
critical_code_review_comments: Hero.jsx: complex per-cell conditional borders on the metrics strip work but are hard to read — could be simplified with grid divide utilities.

Nav.jsx uses Array.prototype.findLast which is ES2023; optional-chained (sections.findLast?.()) so it gracefully no-ops on very old browsers but active-section detection will silently break there. Consider a reduce-based fallback if you need broader browser support.
App.js wraps a single Home route in BrowserRouter — for a GitHub Pages deploy, switching to HashRouter (or configuring 404.html fallback) would avoid hard-refresh 404s on subpaths.
Hero background image is fetched from images.unsplash.com at runtime — for a fully static GitHub Pages site, consider bundling locally to avoid an external dependency and CSP/offline issues.

Contact.jsx silently swallows clipboard errors (catch e -> noop). On insecure contexts or unsupported browsers the user gets no feedback. Consider a toast fallback (sonner is already imported in App.js).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Static React portfolio, no backend, no DB. All content sourced from /app/frontend/src/data/portfolio.js. data-testid coverage is excellent on every interactive and key informational element. PDF served from /app/frontend/public/Krishna_Vamsi_Resume.pdf via Express static. Verified PDF reachable at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL/Krishna_Vamsi_Resume.pdf with content-type application/pdf. All Playwright assertions executed against the public preview URL (https://ai-sde-hub.preview.emergentagent.com).
